observations.recommendation:
1.6 The Audit Team recommended that the LCE require the Municipal
Accountant to provide allowance for impairment on the unsubstantiated
receivables as provided under IPSAS 29 and file a request for authority
to write-off the identified receivables, supported with documents
enumerated in Annex 8 of the COA Circular No. 2023-008 dated August
17, 2023 in order to present fairly the receivables and government equity
accounts reflected in the financial statements.

2.20 We recommended that the LCE:
2.20.1 direct the Municipal Engineer to regularly inform the Municipal
Accountant about the status of infrastructure projects and to
promptly submit all required documents, ensuring that
completed projects unpaid at year-end are properly recorded in
the accounts, as mandated by Section 350 of RA No. 7160.
2.20.2 instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of
its PPE and Inventories and to submit immediately the required
RPCI and RPCPPE to the AT not later than January 31 of each
year as mandated in the aforementioned Section 124, Volume I
of the MNGAS for LGUs.
2.20.3 instruct the Municipal Accountant to record and establish the
correct balances of the PPE accounts and subsequently provide
the corresponding depreciation to reflect the carrying amount
of the affected PPE and to present fairly the FS.

3.4 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile the discrepancy of the
cash balances between the cashbooks and the ledger to reflect the correct
amount of Cash - Local Treasury account in the FS, furthermore,
reconcile the cashbook and ledger at least quarterly or as the need arises
in order to have an accurate, complete and reliable cash balance at any
given time.

4.7 The Audit Team recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare
the corresponding Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to adjust and record
the long outstanding checks by debiting Cash in Bank - LCCA account
and crediting Accounts Payable account in consonance with Section 59
of the MNGAS for LGUs, in order to present accurately the cash in bank
balance in the financial statements.

5.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to record in the agency books all procurement of supplies for
stock as Inventories. Also, require the GSO to receive the purchased
supplies and issue to end users only upon presentation of a duly
accomplished RIS and prepare the weekly SSMI thereof, submit the
same to the Municipal Accountant as basis of the latter in recording the
supplies expense corresponding the total issuances incurred, in order to
easily determine the expense and inventory on hand as of year-end, thus
overstatement or understatement of these accounts is avoided.

6.6 We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Scholarship
Program Screening Committee and Municipal Accountant to review and
verify beneficiaries’ academic records to ensure that these are aligned
with the eligibility requirements and strictly enforce the established
scholarship guidelines consistently to all scholarship recipients, also
revisit the scholarship guidelines and provide possible adjustments on the
eligibility requirements, hence helping more grantees finish their tertiary
education.

7.5 We recommended that Management conduct a thorough review of all
ongoing and planned dredging projects to ensure compliance required
by the above-mentioned regulations, thus, such project/activity goals and
objectives are achieved safely which in turn more viable and beneficial
to the constituents.

8.5 The Audit Team recommended that Management establish strict
monitoring of the timeliness on the renewal of fidelity bond before its
expiration, at least 30 days prior to the expiration date as per TC No. 01-
2020, to ensure protection from possible losses of public funds.

9.9 The LCE:
9.9.1 require only the Municipal Treasurer or a duly authorized and
bonded Special Disbursing Officer to cash advance the
applicable amount requiring distribution to beneficiaries;
9.9.2 submit the lacking documents and strictly enforce direct
payments by check to suppliers in the procurement of goods;
9.9.3 withhold the amount of taxes requiring withholding as required
in the above-cited revenue regulations of law.

10.5 We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Assessor to secure
the necessary documents to support the ownership claims of the
Municipality over the purchased lands. Moreover, instruct the Municipal
Accountant to require all the necessary documents before payment shall
be made to establish the validity and propriety of transactions. Thus,
potential additional expenses in case of going to court for legal cases or
litigation could be avoided.

11.5 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer require the concerned
employees to submit their duly accomplished and certified DTRs,
together with the Travel Orders and Accomplishment Reports, if
applicable, before paying their salaries and wages, hence, payment of the
same is substantiated, thus, attendance and absences of every employee
is properly monitored in compliance with the aforementioned
regulations.

12.7 We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Engineer and the
Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to require the winning bidders the
submission of the CSHP duly approved by the DOLE which shall form
part of the contracts for the implementation of infrastructure projects to
ensure the protection and welfare of the workers and the general public
within and around the immediate vicinity of the construction worksite.

13.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to strictly
adhere to the procurement process mandated in the Revised IRR of RA
9184 and to resort only to alternative modes of procurement when
conditions are appropriately met thus, the integrity and validity of the
transactions would not be compromised.

14.5 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant, Municipal Engineer and the BAC to cause the immediate
submission of the lacking documents observed for all the transactions in
Annex A attached to AOM No. 2025-013 for further evaluation of the
transactions and to avoid suspension in audit. Also, oblige the Municipal
Accountant to ensure that all claims are duly supported with the required
basic documents to ensure validity and propriety of the transactions.

15.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC and the
Supply Officer to strictly adhere to the requirements of COA Circular
No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996 specifically item B therein. Likewise,
advise the BAC to require the dealers/suppliers to indicate the brand
names of the items being quoted in the RFQ which will be the basis of the
BAC in filling-out of the PO and the Supplier for the issuance of Sales
Invoice, thus delivered goods/items could be easily verified and validated.

16.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to submit all the lacking documents and comply with the
deficiencies noted for all the transactions in Annex A attached to AOM
no. 2025-016 to avoid issuance of the Notices of Suspensions. Also, oblige
the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all claims are duly supported
with complete documentation before effecting payment, thus, the
propriety and validity of the transactions could be ascertained.

17.4 We recommended that the LSB ensure strict and diligent compliance
with the timely preparation, submission, and posting of utilization
reports, in accordance with Item 6.1 of DEPED-DBM-DILG JC No. 1,
series of 2017, thus deficiencies, if any, could be communicated to
Management for their immediate corrective action.

18.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor intensify the
implementation of the PPAs funded out from 20% DF and require the
MPDC to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to monitor closely the
implementation thereof so as to deliver to the constituent timely benefits
that could be derived from these projects and to achieve a broader scope
of development. We also recommended Management to
reappropriate/realign projects which are not implementable due to some
constraints including the remaining amounts of projects already
implemented. Moreover, we recommended Management to program
only projects which are attainable and implementable within the
prescribed period, in order to fully implement all the planned and
programmed PPAs.

19.9 The Audit Team recommended that the LCE:
19.9.1 require the concerned implementing offices the prompt
implementation of all projects/programs funded out of the
tobacco excise tax in order to give the constituents and the
tobacco farmers the early benefits that could be derived
therefrom;
19.9.2 advise the Local Development Council and Municipal
Accountant to determine the total unexpended balances of the
completed and unimplemented projects and inform the LCE for
possible reappropriation;
19.9.3 recommend to the Sangguniang Bayan to reappropriate for the
priority projects the funds saved from the fully implemented
and the unimplemented projects that are no longer viable for
implementation hence, funds could be used in accordance with
RA Nos. 7171 and 8240;
19.9.4 ensure that only feasible and implementable PPAs are included
in the Annual Investment Plan;
19.9.5 require regular monitoring of implementation as basis in taking
appropriate action.
19.9.6 Thus, the benefits that could be derived from the
programs/project would be timely and judiciously extended to
the tobacco farmers and other constituents of the LGU

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjustments to take up the cancellation of the stale checks in their respective books of accounts in order to reflect the correct balances of the cash in bank accounts and to restore the corresponding amounts in the Accounts Payable account. Moreover, it is recommended that at least one month before a check becomes stale, the Municipal Treasurer send a written notice to the payee of the existence of the check to facilitate the replacement thereof.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the BAC to comply with the submission of the Final APP to the GPPB on or before the end of January of the budget year and the required posting in the GPPB Website to ensure transparency in the procurement process and in the implementation of procurement contracts.
observations.managementAction: Advised end-user to submit AIP with indicative APP on time.
observations.reason: Submission of the Final APP to the GPPB was done in May 2022.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the BAC to adopt the required procedures and conditions in the Post-Qualification stage particularly in the verification, validation and ascertainment of all statements made and documents submitted by the bidder with the Lowest Calculated Bid to ensure that they are in accordance with the requirements as stated in the Bidding Documents.
observations.managementAction: Advised the TWG to adopt the required procedures and conditions in the Post-Qualification stage.
observations.reason: Some of the required procedures were not carried out due to restrictions brought by COVID-19 protocols.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE enter into a contract with the winning bidder within ten calendar days from receipt of the latter of the Notice of Award to comply with the prescribed period for action on specific procurement activities to ensure the timely implementation of the infrastructure projects, thus benefits from usage of the projects would be promptly given to constituents.
observations.managementAction: Review the Notice of Awards of projects to comply within the Contract of few action.
observations.reason: Contracts for a few projects were not executed within 10 calendar days from receipt by the winning bidder of the Notice of Award.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE oblige the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant to require the concerned contractors who undertook the implementation of the infrastructure projects to post a warranty security in accordance with the required percentage of the total contract price as prescribed under pertinent provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 to guarantee their performance of full responsibilities in case of structural defects and failures from the final acceptance of the infrastructure projects. In addition, require the concerned contractors to secure a certification from the Insurance Company on the validity period of the warranty securities posted.
observations.managementAction: Sent via electronic mail a second notice to the concerned contractors to post the required warranty security.
observations.reason: Some contractors did not yet comply with the deficiencies of their warranty security.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE oblige the General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant to require the concerned suppliers to post a warranty security which shall be covered by either retention money in an amount equivalent to at least one percent (1%) of every progress payment, or a special bank guarantee equivalent to at least one percent (1%) of the total contract to guarantee the performance of their responsibilities in case of manufacturing defects after acceptance of the delivered goods.
observations.managementAction: Immediate inspection of purchases and replacement of defective goods upon acceptance.
observations.reason: No warranty security posted for procurement of goods.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the BAC, through its Secretariat, to consolidate all requisitions of the end-users/implementing units in accordance with the approved APP and subject all procurements to public bidding unless the conditions are present for the use of alternative methods upon approval of the LCE to avoid piece-meal purchases and to ensure transparency and competitiveness in the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: Require end-users to submit requisitions on time.
observations.reason: Some requisitions were not included in the consolidation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Engineer to update
the Municipal Accountant on the status of infrastructure projects and
forward the necessary documents to ensure that completed projects which
remained unpaid as of year-end are recognized in the books of accounts
pursuant to Section 350 of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare
the necessary journal entries to present fairly the affected accounts in the
financial statements.

We recommended that the LCE require:
3.5.1 The Inventory Committee to report the result of the physical count of
PPE using the prescribed forms and follow the instructions on how to
properly accomplish the same to provide the necessary details in the
reconciliation of property and accounting records. Moreover, attach
property tag/sticker for easy identification of government property
and ensure its proper usage.
3.5.2 The Municipal Accountant to maintain complete PPE ledger cards
and continue tracing back the details of prior years’ acquisitions to
support the recorded PPE.
3.5.3 The General Services Officer (GSO) to maintain property cards to
facilitate the physical count of PPE items. Furthermore, determine the
officials/employees to whom the equipment were issued and issue the
corresponding PAR to pinpoint the direct person responsible thereof
and renew them every three years to update the information of the
end-user of the equipment listed in the inventory report. Moreover,
prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable
Property (IIRUP) to account all unserviceable property so that they
may be properly disposed of and dropped from the books of accounts.
Also, prepare and submit to the LCE the necessary report for the
non-existing Slaughterhouse for her appropriate action.
3.5.4 If the existence of PPE in previous years could not be established
despite exhaustion of all diligent efforts, Management may follow the
procedures on the disposition for non-existing PPE items for the one-
time cleansing of PPE account balances of government agencies as
provided under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to
prepare a journal voucher to transfer the cost of the completed projects to
their corresponding Infrastructure Asset accounts from the CIP-
Infrastructure Assets account to reflect their correct balances.

To establish the reliability of the reported Inventories and corresponding
expense accounts, we recommended that the LCE:
5.7.1. Instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of
inventories every year and submit the corresponding report to the
Auditor not later January 31 for verification.
5.7.2. Direct the Municipal Accountant to: (a) observe the Perpetual
Inventory Method of accounting for inventories; (b) maintain supplies
ledger cards; and (c) reconcile regularly with the records maintained
by the Property/Supply Office.
5.7.3. Require the Property/Supply Officer to (a) maintain stock cards; (b)
prepare and submit the SSMI together with the original copies of the
RIS to the Municipal Accountant as basis in recording the issuances
of inventory items; and (c) regularly reconcile with the inventory
records maintained by the Accounting Office.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to
prepare the journal voucher and record the required adjusting entry to
properly reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Engineering Office to
include the cost of project signboard in the preparation of the ABC and
Program of Works and remind the obligation of concerned contractors on
the posting requirement to inform the public of the essential
features/elements of the programs, projects and activities of the
Municipality.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the BAC to require from the
winning bidders the submission of the CSHP duly approved by the DOLE
which shall form part of the contracts for the implementation of
infrastructure projects to ensure the protection and welfare of workers and
the general public within and around the immediate vicinity of the
construction worksite.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to cause
the immediate submission of the lacking documents to facilitate the technical
evaluation of the projects and goods procured and to avoid supension in
audit. Oblige the Municipal Accountant to make sure that all necessary
documents shall be attached to the disbursement voucher before payment is
made to establish the validity and propriety of transactions.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to cause
the immediate submission of the lacking documents and justifications on the
deficiencies observed for further evaluation of the transactions and avoid
suspension in audit. Also, oblige the Municipal Accountant see to it that
disbursement vouchers are duly supported with the required basic
documents to ensure their validity and propriety.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Planning and
Development Coordinator and other concerned offices to coordinate and
strategize the implementation of all the programs/projects embodied under
the 20% DF to attain desirable socio-economic targets and outcomes of the
Municipality and intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that the LCE require the GAD Focal Person to formulate
appropriate programs/projects/activities amidst the COVID-19 pandemic in
order to fully address gender issues/problems of the constituents in the
municipal level and to help the national government attain its goal of
addressing gender issues in the country.

Require the General Services Office to (a) maintain stock cards; (b) prepare and submit the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued together with the original copy of the Requisition Issue Slip to the Municipal Accounting Office as  basis in recording the issuances of inventory items; (c) conduct physical inventory of supplies every semester and submit the corresponding Report of the Physical Count of Inventories to the Auditor concerned not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for appropriate action; and (d) regularly reconcile with the inventory records maintained by the Municipal  Accounting Office.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor  require the Municipal Engineer to see to it that materials testing  for infrastructure projects  be done by  DOST/BRS accredited laboratories to uncover any possible sub-standard construction materials used or components supplied by the contractor  in order to ensure the quality of the projects.  Such cost of materials testing should be shouldered by the contractor as this is included in the computation of the ABC.  Likewise, the Municipal Accountant  Designate should not process claims of contractors without the materials test results.  Moreover, management should conduct inspection of the completed projects before the end of the one year defect liability period and before the issuance of the Final Certificate of Acceptance.  In case there are structural defects attributable to improper construction and/or use of inferior quality materials, the contractor should be held liable.

We have recommended that Management require future contractors to submit  duly accomplished performance security with a validity period that include the one year defects liability period pursuant to Section 39 of the IRR of RA 9184. The validity period shall commence from the effectivity of the contract until after one year from date of completion of the project or until the issuance of the Certificate of Acceptance.  Moreover, the Municipal Accountant being the internal auditor of the municipality should see to it that all supporting documents of disbursement vouchers are properly accomplished to ensure validity and legality of the transactions.
